questions.q: What is the central theme of the poem 'On the Face of It'?
opts: (A) The struggle between fate and will
(B) The importance of love in life
(C) The impact of technology on society
(D) The power of human imagination
questions.explanation: The poem explores the idea of fate and its relationship with human will, making 'The struggle between fate and will' the central theme.

questions.q: Who is the speaker in the poem 'On the Face of It'?
opts: (A) A young girl
(B) An old man
(C) The poet himself
(D) An unknown narrator
questions.explanation: The speaker in the poem is the poet himself, as evident from the first-person narrative.

questions.q: What is the significance of the title 'On the Face of It'?
opts: (A) It highlights the literal meaning of the poem
(B) It emphasizes the surface-level understanding of the poem
(C) It suggests that the poem is about the face of God
(D) It indicates that the poem is a collection of short stories
questions.explanation: The title 'On the Face of It' indicates that the poem is about the surface-level understanding of life, fate, and human will.

questions.q: What is the effect of the use of metaphor in the poem 'On the Face of It'?
opts: (A) It creates a sense of confusion among the readers
(B) It adds depth and complexity to the poem
(C) It makes the poem easier to understand
(D) It has no impact on the readers
questions.explanation: The use of metaphor in the poem adds depth and complexity to the poem, making it more engaging and thought-provoking.

questions.q: What is the speaker's attitude towards fate in the poem 'On the Face of It'?
opts: (A) The speaker is optimistic about fate
(B) The speaker is pessimistic about fate
(C) The speaker is ambivalent towards fate
(D) The speaker is angry with fate
questions.explanation: The speaker in the poem has an ambivalent attitude towards fate, expressing both acceptance and resentment towards it.

questions.q: What is the significance of the image of the 'clock' in the poem 'On the Face of It'?
opts: (A) It represents the passing of time
(B) It symbolizes the power of fate
(C) It indicates the speaker's obsession with time
(D) It has no significance in the poem
questions.explanation: The image of the 'clock' in the poem represents the passing of time, highlighting the speaker's awareness of the fleeting nature of life.

questions.q: What is the effect of the use of irony in the poem 'On the Face of It'?
opts: (A) It creates a sense of humor among the readers
(B) It adds depth and complexity to the poem
(C) It makes the poem more serious and somber
(D) It has no impact on the readers
questions.explanation: The use of irony in the poem adds depth and complexity to the poem, making it more thought-provoking and engaging.

questions.q: What is the speaker's attitude towards the concept of 'fate' in the poem 'On the Face of It'?
opts: (A) The speaker believes in the concept of fate
(B) The speaker rejects the concept of fate
(C) The speaker is ambivalent towards the concept of fate
(D) The speaker is confused about the concept of fate
questions.explanation: The speaker in the poem is ambivalent towards the concept of fate, expressing both acceptance and resentment towards it.
